metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the SSM param (terraform apply) must only be destroyed AFTER the fetch-secrets.sh code change has been merged to main AND autodeploy has confirmed the updated script is live on the box — or temporarily convert the autolander-gerrit-token fetch to get_param_optional in a prep commit before the SSM destroy.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh fetches autolander-gerrit-token with get_param (REQUIRED/fail-fast). If the SSM param is destroyed by terraform apply before the code change removing that fetch is deployed to the box, any subsequent .env regeneration (on reboot or triggered by autodeploy) will abort with exit 1, breaking the whole surviving stack.
findings.location: ## What (live) — ordering of repo edits vs. terraform apply; infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh line 99

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default definition of 'quiet window' in the plan (e.g., 'weekday off-peak hours, no active deployment pipeline running, on-call acknowledged') so the executor can act without clarification.
findings.finding: 'Frontier creds, quiet window' is undefined — 'quiet window' is not anchored to specific hours, criteria, or a named maintenance schedule, leaving the executor to guess what conditions must be met before running terraform apply.
findings.location: ## What (live) — 'terraform apply: destroy … (via the deleted .tf) — frontier creds, quiet window'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'quiet window' with the concrete criterion, e.g.: '— apply during off-peak hours (weekday 02:00–06:00 local) with no active deploy pipeline and on-call engineer notified.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / rollback section covering at minimum: (a) snapshot or export the SSM param value before deletion, (b) note that volume data should be confirmed empty/stale before removal, and (c) document the terraform state import commands or manual re-creation steps if apply fails mid-run.
findings.finding: The plan contains multiple irreversible destructive operations (volume deletion, SSM param deletion, CloudWatch alarm deletion) with no explicit rollback or back-out path, violating T4's requirement that the remedy be a rollback/back-out plan, not mere acknowledgement.
findings.location: What (live — operator, staged, careful) + Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback / Back-out' section: '(1) Before deleting the SSM param, copy its value to a local secure note (re-creatable via aws ssm put-parameter). (2) Confirm gerrit_autolander* volumes contain no unprocessed/unreplicated data (docker run --rm ... ls). (3) If terraform apply fails mid-run, run terraform plan to identify remaining resources and re-run apply; if alarm must be restored, use terraform import or re-add the .tf temporarily.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback clause: before deleting volumes, snapshot or document their contents; before deleting the SSM param, record the token value in a temporary secure location (or confirm the service is provably idle), so the decommission can be reversed within a defined window if needed.
findings.finding: The live-ops section permanently deletes docker volumes (gerrit_autolander, gerrit_autolander_tickets) and the SSM autolander-gerrit-token credential with no specified rollback procedure. If the team needs to restore the service (e.g., it turns out it is still needed), the volumes' contents are unrecoverable and the Gerrit token must be re-issued through an out-of-band credential workflow.
findings.location: ## What (live — operator, staged, careful) — volume and SSM deletion steps
findings.suggested_fix: Insert a pre-destruction step: (1) `docker run --rm -v gerrit_autolander:/data alpine tar czf /backup/gerrit_autolander_$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz /data` to snapshot volumes; (2) note the SSM param ARN and confirm with the Gerrit team the token can be revoked-not-reissued, or keep it in AWS Secrets Manager for 30 days before final deletion. Add a rollback section: 'To restore: re-create volumes from snapshot, re-issue Gerrit token, re-apply the deleted .tf files.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an operator step to revoke (delete or rotate) the Gerrit HTTP password for the RebarBotNava bot account in the Gerrit admin UI (or via `DELETE /a/accounts/self/password.http`) as part of the live decommission sequence, so the credential is dead on both the SSM and Gerrit sides.
findings.finding: The plan destroys the SSM SecureString copy of the autolander Gerrit token but does not declare whether the actual Gerrit HTTP password for the bot account (RebarBotNava) is revoked in Gerrit itself, leaving a live, un-revoked credential with rebase-on-behalf and submit privileges on a public-internet-reachable Gerrit REST API.
findings.location: What (live — operator, staged, careful) — terraform apply step / infra/terraform/ssm.tf line 57
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'What (live)': '[ ] Gerrit admin: delete or rotate the HTTP password for the RebarBotNava account (Gerrit Settings → HTTP Credentials → Delete, or DELETE /a/accounts/{id}/password.http) to ensure the autolander-gerrit-token credential is revoked at the source, not just removed from SSM.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `terraform plan -out=tfplan` review step (and operator sign-off on the diff) before `terraform apply` in the live section.
findings.finding: The live section jumps directly to 'terraform apply' without an explicit 'terraform plan' step to review the destroy diff before executing it.
findings.suggested_fix: Insert before the apply bullet: 'terraform plan -out=tfplan — confirm the diff shows ONLY the aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.autolander_errors destroy and the aws_ssm_parameter.rebar_secrets["/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token"] destroy, then terraform apply tfplan.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the autoheal conditionality: either make the live step match the repo step's 'if only for it' gate, or add an explicit pre-check (e.g., 'verify autoheal has no other watched services before removing') that applies to both sections.
findings.finding: The autoheal removal is conditional in the repo section ('if only for it') but unconditional in the live section, creating a cross-section contradiction that would send the implementer in two directions at execution time.
findings.location: What (repo) docker-compose.yml vs What (live) docker compose step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a shared pre-condition note: 'Confirm autoheal watches only autolander (inspect docker-compose.yml labels/depends_on). If confirmed, remove it in both repo and live steps; otherwise retain it in both.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before deleting the SSM param, audit all callers (grep repo for `autolander-gerrit-token`, check CloudTrail GetParameter events for the past 30 days) and confirm no consumer other than the autolander service references it.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate consumers of the SSM parameter `autolander-gerrit-token` beyond the autolander service itself. If any CI pipeline, script, or other service reads this parameter, deleting it will cause a silent breakage that is not caught by the stated acceptance criteria ('terraform plan is empty, lint/typecheck/test green').
findings.location: infra/terraform — ssm.tf / ## What (live) SSM param deletion
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-deletion audit step: `aws cloudtrail lookup-events --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=ResourceName,AttributeValue=autolander-gerrit-token --start-time $(date -d '30 days ago' --iso-8601)` to enumerate all callers, and cross-reference with a repo-wide grep.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` to the repo deletion list (it is the autolander's Gerrit ACL smoke-check script and has no consumer after decommission).
findings.finding: infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh is not listed for deletion. The file exists solely to verify autolander Gerrit ACL/credential provisioning (it has no other consumer) and becomes unreachable dead-script surface once the autolander is decommissioned.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `- [ ] infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh — delete.` to the What (repo) section alongside the other file deletions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/autolander/` (the Python package) and `tests/autolander/` (its unit suite) to the repo deletion list, since both become dead code once the container and all deployment paths are removed.
findings.finding: The plan does not list the deletion of infra/autolander/ (the Python package: loop.py, land.py, failure.py, gerrit.py, etc.) or tests/autolander/ (its unit-test suite). These become dead code with zero consumers once the Dockerfile, compose service, and all deployment paths are removed — a retained abstraction with no remaining call-site.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to What (repo): `- [ ] infra/autolander/ — delete entire directory (Python package has no runtime caller without the container).` and `- [ ] tests/autolander/ — delete entire directory (tests have no subject under test).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the `fetch-secrets.sh` change to also remove the `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` line (line 116), not just the `autolander-gerrit-token` fetch, so no autolander keys remain in the generated .env.
findings.finding: infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh writes `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` to the .env file (line 116) in addition to the `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` line. The plan's fetch-secrets.sh item only mentions removing the gerrit-token fetch, leaving the port constant as a dangling, unused .env key.
findings.suggested_fix: In fetch-secrets.sh, delete both the `autolander_gerrit_token=$(get_param autolander-gerrit-token)` fetch (lines ~99) and the `echo "AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081"` emission (line 116).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/autolander/` directory to the repo deletion list (delete the entire package: __init__.py, loop.py, failure.py, gerrit.py, land.py).
findings.finding: The plan does not include deletion of the infra/autolander/ source package directory (containing __init__.py, loop.py, failure.py, gerrit.py, land.py), which exists in the repo and is referenced only by the autolander container being decommissioned.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: '- [ ] infra/autolander/ — delete the entire package directory (loop.py, failure.py, gerrit.py, land.py, __init__.py).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Also remove the `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` emit line (and its comment) from fetch-secrets.sh, and update the nginx/rebar.conf.template envsubst header to drop the AUTOLANDER_PORT reference.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'remove autolander-gerrit-token fetch' from fetch-secrets.sh, but the file also emits AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081 as a non-secret port line (line 116). The nginx template header (lines 4-11) also references AUTOLANDER_PORT in its envsubst invocation example. Both must be cleaned up, but neither is named in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the fetch-secrets.sh bullet: 'remove autolander-gerrit-token fetch AND the AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081 emit line + comment.' Extend the nginx bullet: 'remove the /autolander/status location block AND update the envsubst header comment to drop ${AUTOLANDER_PORT}.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` to the repo deletion list.
findings.finding: infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh exists in the repo and reads AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN from the environment, but is not mentioned in the plan's deletion list.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: '- [ ] infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh — delete (autolander-specific provisioning smoke-check, no longer applicable).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Also remove the `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` output line (and its header comment) from `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, not just the `autolander-gerrit-token` fetch.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh line 116 (`echo "AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081"`) is an unenumerated edit target: removing only the autolander-gerrit-token fetch (line 99) leaves AUTOLANDER_PORT still emitted into the .env, writing a dead variable that nothing consumes once the autolander service is removed.
findings.location: What (repo) — infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh / plan item 'remove autolander-gerrit-token fetch'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove lines 24 (`AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` mention in comment), 99 (`autolander_gerrit_token` fetch), 114 (`AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN=...` echo), and 116 (`AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` echo) from fetch-secrets.sh.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the `infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template` header comment and envsubst invocation example (lines 4-11) to remove all references to `${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` when the `/autolander/status` location block is deleted.
findings.finding: The nginx rebar.conf.template header comment (lines 4-11) references `${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` as a required envsubst variable alongside `${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}`. The plan says to 'remove the /autolander/status location' but does not mention updating the template header or the envsubst invocation example, which will be left referencing a variable that no longer appears in the template body.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the envsubst line in the header to `envsubst '${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}'` and remove `${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` from the comment's variable list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In `fetch-secrets.sh`, also remove the `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` hardcoded line (and its associated comment referencing the autolander port) alongside the `autolander-gerrit-token` fetch.
findings.finding: fetch-secrets.sh emits `AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081` (line 116) as a hardcoded non-secret line whose only consumer is the removed autolander service and nginx location. The plan's fetch-secrets.sh item says only to 'remove autolander-gerrit-token fetch', leaving AUTOLANDER_PORT as a stale entry written to every regenerated .env after decommission.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the `echo "AUTOLANDER_PORT=8081"` line and update the comment at lines 24-25 to remove the AUTOLANDER_PORT mention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] When removing the `/autolander/status` location block from `rebar.conf.template`, also update the header comment (lines 4–11) to remove all references to `${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` and the autolander port export from the envsubst example.
findings.finding: The nginx template header (lines 4–11 of rebar.conf.template) documents `${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` as a substitution variable in its envsubst example comment. The plan removes only the `/autolander/status` location block body, leaving the header comment referencing AUTOLANDER_PORT — which becomes a stale, misleading instruction after decommission.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `${REVIEW_BOT_PORT} and ${AUTOLANDER_PORT}` with `${REVIEW_BOT_PORT}` in line 4, remove `AUTOLANDER_PORT` from the `export` line (line 6) and the `envsubst` argument (line 7), and update line 11 to read 'Only ${REVIEW_BOT_PORT} is templated'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live step to pause (or verify the quiescence of) the rebar-autodeploy.timer during the decommission window, resuming it only after the repo changes have been deployed and verified.
findings.finding: autodeploy.sh runs every ~2 minutes and will attempt to rebuild and restart the autolander service if it detects AUTOLANDER_PATHS changes between the deployed SHA and main. Stopping the container (live step 1) without pausing autodeploy can cause autodeploy to re-raise it before the code changes removing the service have been deployed.
findings.location: ## What (live) — docker compose stop+remove bullet; infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh lines 47–48, 206–242
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the live section: 'Before stopping the autolander container: `systemctl stop rebar-autodeploy.timer` (re-enable after code changes are confirmed live: `systemctl start rebar-autodeploy.timer`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Determine whether `autoheal` is exclusively used by the autolander service and record the decision in the plan (either 'remove autoheal' unconditionally or 'keep autoheal, remove only its autolander-specific rules') so the executor has no conditional to resolve.
findings.finding: The compose step 'remove autoheal sidecar (if only for it)' is a conditional that defers to the executor the investigation of whether autoheal serves other containers. The executor must effectively 'check if' autoheal is autolander-only before deciding to remove it — a real design decision the plan does not settle.
findings.location: ## What (repo) — infra/compose/docker-compose.yml bullet; ## What (live) — docker compose step
findings.suggested_fix: Inspect the current docker-compose.yml for autoheal's `AUTOHEAL_CONTAINER_LABEL` / service list. If autoheal targets only autolander, change the bullet to 'remove autoheal sidecar.' If it targets others, change it to 'remove autolander from autoheal's monitored set; keep the sidecar.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decide whether to delete or update `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` — it references `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` and autolander Gerrit ACL checks that become stale after the token SSM param is removed.
findings.finding: infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh is an unenumerated consumer: it references AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN (used as a credential for live Gerrit REST checks), the Autosubmit label, and autolander ACL provisioning. After the token SSM param is deleted, any operator running this script will get a credential-not-found failure with no guidance. The file is not named in the plan.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
